mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, which increases equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of synchronized growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 210.0 indicates deflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain ultra-loose policy, which supports equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL stays below 210.0, or the threshold effect disappears when tested against a continuous linear model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which boosts household income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or 60-day forward returns underperform the risk-free rate in more than 55 percent of aligned labor market months.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market stabilization phase, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal renewed construction demand and wealth effect recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of stabilization-phase months, or HOUST growth fails to correlate with equity returns once mortgage rates are controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When real sales of autos and light trucks exceed 340,000 units, consumer durables demand confirms spending resilience, which reduces recession fears and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where RSAFS exceeds 340,000, or the threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
